question: What pricing trends can be observed for various car categories over different production years?

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap visualizes multivariate relationships and is particularly effective in highlighting patterns of price concentration across categories and years. By binning 'Prod. year' (e.g., into 5-year intervals to reduce sparseness) and aggregating median 'Price' for each (category, time bin), the chart exposes which categories and time windows correspond to higher/lower typical prices. The color intensity directly encodes price, providing an unconventional but highly readable summary view across multiple car categories and their evolution in the market.
analysis.target_insight: This visualization shows the relative pricing (median) for each car category across grouped production year intervals, exposing category-specific peaks, troughs, or stable periods in price over time.

chart_encodings.color: Median Price for (Category, Year Interval) shown via colorbar
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Custom colormap (continuous viridis), annotating each cell for median price, clear colorbar, rotated x-axis labels

transformations: Bin 'Prod. year' into 5-year intervals using pd.cut.
Group by 'Category' and binned 'Prod. year', aggregate 'Price' as median.
Pivot to create a matrix with 'Category' as rows and year bins as columns.
data_preparation_steps: Read in dataset from csv_c_squared\28.csv using pandas.
Create 5-year bins for 'Prod. year' from min to max using pd.cut; label bins as intervals.
Pivot data with 'Category' index and 'Prod. year_bin' columns.
Prepare matrix for heatmap and set labels.
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv("csv_c_squared\28.csv")
prod_year_min = df["Prod. year"].min()
prod_year_max = df["Prod. year"].max()
bin_width = 5
bins = np.arange(prod_year_min, prod_year_max+bin_width, bin_width)
labels = [f"{int(bins[i])}-{int(bins[i+1]-1)}" for i in range(len(bins)-1)]
df["Prod. year_bin"] = pd.cut(df["Prod. year"], bins=bins, labels=labels, include_lowest=True)
df_group = df.groupby(["Category", "Prod. year_bin"], as_index=False)["Price"].median()
df_pivot = df_group.pivot(index="Category", columns="Prod. year_bin", values="Price")
cmap = mpl.colormaps["viridis"]
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 8))
im = plt.imshow(df_pivot.values, aspect="auto", cmap=cmap, interpolation="nearest")
plt.title("Car Category Pricing Heatmap by Production Year Interval")
plt.xlabel("Production Year Interval")
plt.ylabel("Category")
plt.xticks(ticks=np.arange(len(df_pivot.columns)), labels=list(df_pivot.columns), rotation=45)
plt.yticks(ticks=np.arange(len(df_pivot.index)), labels=list(df_pivot.index))
plt.colorbar(im, label="Median Price")
# Annotate each cell with price
for i in range(df_pivot.shape[0]):
    for j in range(df_pivot.shape[1]):
        price = df_pivot.values[i, j]
        if not np.isnan(price):
            plt.text(j, i, f"{int(price)}", ha="center", va="center", color="white" if price > np.nanmedian(df_pivot.values) else "black", fontsize=8)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
